untranslatable_reason: This signal is a framework for calculating annual returns (year-end closing price comparisons) rather than a traditional entry/exit trading rule. It requires date manipulation (identifying last day of December, last day of January, year transitions) and comparative analysis across 12-month periods. The allowed indicators and operators do not support: (1) date-based logic (month == 12, next_day.month == 1), (2) temporal alignment checks (ensuring January after December), (3) year-over-year return calculations. The signal establishes a measurement framework (EVENT category) rather than actionable trading conditions with specific entry/exit points.
translation_notes: Signal ID CHAN_QT_DRY_0 appears designed for backtesting infrastructure to measure annual performance metrics rather than generate trading signals. To implement this, the backtest engine would need: (1) date parsing capabilities, (2) annual return calculation logic separate from entry/exit rules, and (3) regime framework adapted for year-end measurement cycles. Recommend implementing as a separate 'metrics calculation' rule type rather than a standard signal rule.
